name: Zipline-Reloaded (Python Backtesting Engine)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); community fork maintained by Stefan Jansen

auth.notes: Local library; data bundles require separate data source

performance.notes: Event-driven simulation; performance depends on universe size and lookback

agent_readiness.notes: Maintained fork of Quantopian's Zipline backtester. Event-driven execution model. @api_calls: handle_data(context, data), initialize(context), before_trading_start. zipline run -f algo.py -b quandl. Data bundles: quandl, quantopian-quandl, custom. Integrated with pyfolio for performance analysis. Realistic transaction cost modeling (slippage, commissions). Familiar to ex-Quantopian users. More complex setup than vectorbt but more realistic simulation.

gotchas: Complex data bundle setup and ingestion
Python 3.10+ compatibility issues in some versions
Quandl free data deprecated; need alternative data sources
best_when: Realistic event-driven backtesting with transaction costs
Research requiring handle_data loop simulation style
Integration with pyfolio for tearsheet generation
avoid_when: When data bundle setup is a blocker
summary: Zipline-Reloaded is the community-maintained fork of Quantopian's Zipline event-driven backtesting engine. handle_data(context, data) loop, realistic slippage/commission modeling, data bundles. Integrates with pyfolio for performance tearsheets. Familiar to ex-Quantopian users. More realistic simulation than vectorized approaches but steeper setup. Use vectorbt for speed, Zipline for realism.
